introduction: A heavy oak desk sits in the corner, covered in arcane tomes, scrolls, and alchemical equipment. This is Marcus Nightshade's personal study.
The books are ancient - some bear the Nightshade family crest. Others are bound in materials you don't want to identify. The air here feels heavy with residual magic.
On the desk, you see an open journal, a half-finished letter, and forbidden tomes.
You open the journal to a recent entry:
'I can feel IT in my mind now. The Shadow Blight whispers to me, shows me visions of what the Aethel discovered. Power beyond imagination. The ability to transcend mortality itself.'
'But the price... Cassia wouldn't understand. She clings to the old ways, to compassion and mercy. She doesn't see that those virtues are chains, holding us back from our true potential.'
'I've made my choice. The ritual will proceed. If she tries to stop me... I'll do what must be done. High Cultist Taborex has shown me the path.'
The letter is addressed to someone named 'Brother Aldric' in a distant city:
'The final phase begins tonight. The sacrifices are complete, the barrier weakened. My family's bloodline - the key Taborex needed - will open the portal.'
'Once the Shadow Blight flows through fully, our organization will control power beyond mortal comprehension. The kingdoms will fall. The corrupted shall inherit the earth.'
'I regret only one thing: Cassia. She was the light to my darkness, and I loved her for it. But love is weakness. The Blight has shown me this truth.'
'If she comes for me, I will not hesitate. The work is too important.'
The letter is unsigned and unmailed. He hasn't sent it yet.
The books on Marcus's desk are deeply disturbing:
'Rituals of Dimensional Binding' - An Aethel tome describing how to open and stabilize portal-stones.
'The Shadow Protocols' - Pages filled with corrupted Aethel glyphs that seem to writhe when you're not looking directly at them.
'Nightshade Family Grimoire' - Marcus's own ancestral spellbook, now defaced with shadow corruption sigils.
'Fragments of the Arcane Collapse' - A historical account of the Aethel's final days, with certain passages heavily annotated.
One passage in the Aethel tome is bookmarked, with Marcus's notes in the margin:

Original text: 'The Old Blood carries the resonance. Those descended from the First Kings of the pre-Collapse era can attune to dormant nexus points. Their bloodline acts as a living key to the portal-stones.'

Marcus's note: 'The Nightshade line traces back to the Aethel nobility. Taborex was right - I am the key. My blood can awaken the portal. But do I sacrifice myself... or bind myself to the Blight as its vessel? Power beyond imagining...'

A chill runs down your spine. Marcus isn't just a cultist - he's a necessary component of the ritual itself. His bloodline is the key to opening the ancient Aethel portal and releasing the Shadow Blight.

options.text: [Something in the Aethel records makes your blood run cold...]

introduction: You find a secondary passage near the back of the Aethel tome — unmarked, unread by Marcus.
Elara steps close, her voice low.
'Wait. Look at this section. 'Cavia subject cohorts — resonance attuned through iterative exposure. First generation loss acceptable.'
She closes her eyes for a moment.
'Your ancestors... they were test subjects for the Aethel dimensional experiments. The Cavia did not just live through the Collapse — they were used to cause it. I found the records. I'm so sorry.'

options.text: [Defiant] 'Then we end this here. No one else gets used.'
[Resigned] 'I always wondered why we were different. Now I know.'
